{"idea": {"Name": "geometric_icl_compute_control", "Title": "Task Manifold Projection: Training-Free Geometric Filtering for Robust In-Context Learning and Dynamic Compute Allocation", "Short Hypothesis": "In-context learning operates within a low-dimensional activation subspace ('task manifold') spanned by demonstrations, while distributional shifts inject orthogonal noise that degrades performance. Standard prompt engineering or post-hoc filtering cannot disentangle overlapping semantic and noise components in high-dimensional space. A frozen LLM's residual stream provides a direct, training-free window into this geometry, making it the ideal setting to test whether linear subspace projection can isolate task-relevant features and whether the geometric stability of this manifold across layers can predict inference readiness for dynamic compute allocation.", "Related Work": "Prior work on ICL focuses on scaling laws, prompt optimization, or training-ba
